title: Embeddings, Vector Spaces, Similarity, and Model Contracts · Program Brief: Rag Embedding Purpose Model Card
summary: A team mixes query vectors from one model with document vectors from another, changes cosine to dot product, truncates dimensions, and interprets closeness as factual support. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the embedding purpose model card increment in embedding-space and similarity audit. Learners progress…
objectives: Select an embedding model using its training objective, query/document prompts, languages, domains, dimensions, license, limits, and evaluated task fit.
Use paired per-query differences, confidence intervals or resampling, practical thresholds, and regression inspection when comparing retrievers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example RAG engineering team handles case 5723 during an incident-response knowledge search. Build deterministic Python evidence to trace embedding space, dimensions, similarity, normalization, and model-version boundaries; keep every status keyboard reachable, announced, readable without color, and linked to structured source evidence; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select an embedding model using its training objective, query/document prompts, languages, domains, dimensions, license, limits, and evaluated task fit. A defensible solution applies Rag Embedding Purpose Model Card, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to embedding-space and similarity audit.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-embeddings-vector-semantics-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-embeddings-vector-semantics-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_embeddings_vector_semantics_4(query: tuple[float, ...] = (1.0, 0.0, 1.0), document: tuple[float, ...] = (0.5, 0.0, 0.5), dimensions: int = 3, same_model: bool = True, normalized: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if dimensions <= 0 or len(query) != dimensions or len(document) != dimensions:
        return False, 0.0, "dimension-contract"
    if not same_model:
        return False, 0.0, "embedding-space"
    dot = sum(left * right for left, right in zip(query, document, strict=True))
    query_norm = sum(value * value for value in query) ** 0.5
    document_norm = sum(value * value for value in document) ** 0.5
    if query_norm == 0.0 or document_norm == 0.0:
        return False, 0.0, "zero-vector"
    cosine = dot / (query_norm * document_norm)
    if normalized and abs(query_norm - 1.0) > 1e-6:
        return False, cosine, "normalization-claim"
    assert -1.0 <= cosine <= 1.0
    return True, round(cosine, 4), "cosine-evidence"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python RAG decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected query or corpus case, then state which native model, vector-store, provider, network, load, privacy, security, recovery, or deployment claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.
